[Contributors 2](https://github.com/danicat/testquery/graphs/contributors). *   [Go 95.9%](https://github.com/danicat/testquery/search?l=go). *   [Makefile 4.1%](https://github.com/danicat/testquery/search?l=makefile). *   [Contact](https://support.github.com/?tags=dotcom-footer).", "score": 0.8571119, "raw_content": null}, {"url": "https://portal.rfgen.com/help/6.0/RFgen6_Help/Content/RFgenStudio/To%20Test%20SQL%20Queries.htm?TocPath=RFgen%20Mobile%20Development%20Studio%7CTesting%7C_____3", "title": "To Test SQL Queries - RFgen", "content": "The **Execute** option tests SQL statements so you can see results before executing the statement in code. Any SQL command entered here is submitted to the ODBC driver for execution. The **Options** > **Use ERP Connection** function allows you to enable a connection to a specific ERP which simplifies the process of executing a SQL statement against an ERP connection such as SAP, Oracle JDE, Oracle EBS, Deltek CostPoint, or Microsoft Dynamics. To test the query, enable the ERP mode (slide box to the right), enter your statement in the query box and press Execute. As transactions are applied against your data, you will need to re-execute your SQL statement. In this case, highlight the intended SQL statement and click Execute from the menu. Most generic queries can be entered and executed for any brand of SQL. The **SQL History** option allows you to select from any of the previously executed SQL commands and to also limit the output to a maximum number of records.", "score": 0.7265231, "raw_content": null}, {"url": "https://stackoverflow.com/questions/3668506/efficient-sql-test-query-or-validation-query-that-will-work-across-all-or-most", "title": "Efficient SQL test query or validation query that will work across all ...", "content": "This is the query that will be executed just before a connection is given to you from the pool to validate that the connection to the database is still alive.", "score": 0.6628188, "raw_content": null}, {"url": "https://dba.stackexchange.com/questions/223425/how-to-test-query", "title": "how to test query - Database Administrators Stack Exchange", "content": "Still be very careful with this in production or shared dev/test/other instances though: long-running updates will still consume resources (IO, CPU, memory) so may slow down other users and locks will be held as needed so you can still cause the delays and other issues (potentially deadlocks) that your data changing statements normally would. To test the result you need not to `delete` smth at all,. While it can be not a problem to `begin tran`, do a `delete` and then `rollback` for a table with 10 rows in test environment, it can be a problem on large volumes of data or in concurrent environment, where you'll `lock` your table in absence of `index` on `colname`, and `delete` (and its `rollback`) will generate a lot of `transaction log` since `delete` is `fully logged operation` in any recovery model. **Reminder:** Answers generated by AI tools are not allowed due to Database Administrators Stack Exchange's artificial intelligence policy. Thanks for contributing an answer to Database Administrators Stack Exchange!", "score": 0.5920305, "raw_content": null}, {"url": "https://www.reddit.com/r/SQL/comments/1kpo7ci/how_do_you_test_sql_queries/", "title": "How do you test SQL queries? - Reddit", "content": "# How do you test SQL queries? I know there isn't really a standard SQL testing framework but at work, we currently run tests on queries through Pytest against databases set up in containers. I'm more interested in the way you typically set up your mocks and structure your tests. Each table is populated with all combinations of data that will test different parts of the query. For every query tested, the database is therefore set up the exact same way. For every test, the query results would therefore also be identical. I just set up different test functions that assert on the different conditions of the result that we're interested in. It's not entirely consistent across the org but the pattern more closely resembles every test having their own specific set of mocks. ## Top Posts. * reReddit: Top posts of May 18, 2025. reReddit: Top posts of May 2025. reReddit: Top posts of 2025.", "score": 0.5852203, "raw_content": null}]}
